last_result.message: A structural recovery boundary is available for the current committed branch.

checkpoint_options.label: Before latest call-obligation tactic #45
checkpoint_options.why_checkpoint: call-obligation local boundary; selecting it keeps the opened call obligation and removes later local work
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the recent local step appears to have introduced the current blocker.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe a smaller replacement step or inspect the current goal before recommitting.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #45 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.label: After seq opened / before branch work #23
checkpoint_options.why_checkpoint: seq-local branch boundary; selecting it keeps the seq cut and removes branch-local work after it
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the recent local step appears to have introduced the current blocker.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe a smaller replacement step or inspect the current goal before recommitting.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #23 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.label: After call opened / before obligation work #6
checkpoint_options.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the recent local step appears to have introduced the current blocker.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe a smaller replacement step or inspect the current goal before recommitting.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #6 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.label: Before call invariant #5
checkpoint_options.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the current blocker is an invariant gap, a bad-event fact that should have been preserved, or a local surgery problem after a call. Prefer this over fresh restart when the top-level route still looks right.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Inspect `call_subgoals` or `subgoal_gap`, then probe a revised `call (_: ...)` invariant or a smaller prefix.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #5 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

options.label: Continue current branch
options.effect_if_selected: This keeps working from the current proof state.

options.label: Show all rewind choices
options.effect_if_selected: This shows committed tactics you can rewind before.

options.label: Erase branch and restart
options.effect_if_selected: This confirms erasing committed tactics in this node and restarting from the target lemma.

surface_profile.name: l1_goal_projection
surface_profile.description: L1 Goal-State Projection: manager transport of ONLY the current goal. No status, no manager-result section, no focus — the agent sees the raw goal and nothing else (the node-memory recovery anchor and the one-line submit-intent reminder are the only scaffolding).
